📋 Changelog Automation

Latest Edition · Updated

Automating your changelog and release notes saves time, ensures consistency, and keeps your users informed. This guide covers the tools, conventions, and workflows for hands-free release documentation.

Why Automate Changelogs?

  • Eliminates manual release note writing (error-prone, tedious)
  • Ensures every change is documented — nothing slips through
  • Maintains consistent formatting across all releases
  • Reduces release cycle time by removing manual steps
  • Enables continuous delivery with generated changelogs per commit

Conventional Commits Foundation

Automated changelogs work best when your team follows Conventional Commits:

feat: add dark mode support
fix: resolve login redirect loop
docs: update API reference for v3
refactor: simplify database connection pooling
perf: optimise image loading with lazy evaluation
test: add unit tests for payment module
chore: update CI configuration for Node 22

Each commit type maps to a changelog section:

  • feat → Features
  • fix → Bug Fixes
  • docs → Documentation
  • perf → Performance
  • BREAKING CHANGE → Breaking Changes (triggers major version bump)

Popular Tools

Semantic Release

Fully automated version management and package publishing:

# .releaserc.json
{
  "branches": ["main"],
  "plugins": [
    "@semantic-release/commit-analyzer",
    "@semantic-release/release-notes-generator",
    "@semantic-release/changelog",
    "@semantic-release/npm",
    "@semantic-release/git"
  ]
}

Changesets

Designed for monorepos with per-package changelogs:

# Add a changeset
npx changeset

# Version packages
npx changeset version

# Publish all changed packages
npx changeset publish

Release Please

Creates release PRs automatically from conventional commits:

# release-please-config.json
{
  "packages": {
    ".": {
      "release-type": "node",
      "changelog-path": "CHANGELOG.md"
    }
  }
}

Best Practices

  • Enforce conventional commits with a commit-msg hook (commitlint)
  • Generate changelogs in CI — never manually edit CHANGELOG.md
  • Include migration guides for breaking changes
  • Link to relevant issues and PRs in release notes
  • Use pre-release channels (alpha, beta, next) for testing
  • Archive old changelogs for long-running projects

Common Pitfalls

  • ❌ Mixing conventional and non-conventional commits
  • ❌ Forgetting to include scope in monorepo commits
  • ❌ Not documenting breaking changes with BREAKING CHANGE: footer
  • ❌ Manual version bumps that conflict with automation
  • ❌ Skipping changelog generation for "small" releases
